channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58381/
Subject: LU-17950 ldiskfs: race in ext4_inode_attach_jinode
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 10fa969f87c2c4306a2da2087008f765f8cd0627

"Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58381
Subject: LU-17950 ldiskfs: race in ext4_inode_attach_jinode
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1631d3182d33e314fe594174ac1692c47d8f07e5

I'm not sure if it's related to LU-17249.
I've found the jbd2_inode on the trnsaction->t_inode_list after the problematic ff1ffc7b0852aa00

crash> struct jbd2_inode ff1ffc756344fbc0
struct jbd2_inode {
  i_transaction = 0xff1ffc779ee5e600, 
  i_next_transaction = 0x0, 
  i_list = {
    next = 0xff1ffc756344fc10, 
    prev = 0xff1ffc7b0852aa10
  }, 
  i_vfs_inode = 0xff1ffc771cbbf6f8, 
  i_flags = 6, 
  i_dirty_start = 0, 
  i_dirty_end = 8191
}

so the prev does point back to ff1ffc7b0852aa10. and I can iterate the list from there.

It confirms that the jbd2_inode ff1ffc7b0852aa00 was on the list there's even an element point back to it,
and somehow the content got initialized again by jbd2_journal_init_jbd_inode().

However the code doesn't support the case of double free and use after it. We only free the jbd2_inode in ldiskfs_clear_inode() and that's refcount protected by iput()>evict()>ldiskfs_evict_inode(), so I'm running out of ideas here.
discussed on triage call. Possibly related to LU-17249. For now, consider this likely to be a pre-existing rare issue and remove from scope for 2.15.5

So I'm still investigating but here is what I found so far:
we crashed when adding the journal inode to the transaction's t_inode_list:

	list_add(&jinode->i_list, &transaction->t_inode_list);

crash> struct jbd2_journal_handle ff1ffc6c80aa6038
struct jbd2_journal_handle {
  {
    h_transaction = 0xff1ffc779ee5e600, 
    h_journal = 0xff1ffc779ee5e600
  }, 
  h_rsv_handle = 0x0, 
  h_total_credits = 109, 
  h_revoke_credits = 8, 
  h_revoke_credits_requested = 8, 
  h_ref = 1, 
  h_err = 0, 
  h_sync = 0, 
  h_jdata = 0, 
  h_reserved = 0, 
  h_aborted = 0, 
  h_type = 0, 
  h_line_no = 1990, 
  h_start_jiffies = 4507393842, 
  h_requested_credits = 113, 
  saved_alloc_context = 0
}
crash> struct transaction_s ff1ffc779ee5e600
struct transaction_s {
  t_journal = 0xff1ffc6c117b9800, 
  t_tid = 100455169, 
  t_state = T_RUNNING, 
  t_log_start = 0, 
  t_nr_buffers = 157, 
  t_reserved_list = 0x0, 
  t_buffers = 0xff1ffc7593c972d0, 
  t_forget = 0x0, 
  t_checkpoint_list = 0x0, 
  t_checkpoint_io_list = 0x0, 
  t_shadow_list = 0x0, 
  t_inode_list = {
    next = 0xff1ffc7b0852aa10, 
    prev = 0xff1ffc782c359b50
  }, 
  t_handle_lock = {
    {
      rlock = {
        raw_lock = {
          {
            val = {
              counter = 0
            }, 
            {
              locked = 0 '\000', 
              pending = 0 '\000'
            }, 
            {
              locked_pending = 0, 
              tail = 0
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }, 
  t_max_wait = 0, 
  t_start = 4507393634, 
  t_requested = 4507393660, 
  t_chp_stats = {
    cs_chp_time = 0, 
    cs_forced_to_close = 0, 
    cs_written = 0, 
    cs_dropped = 0
  }, 
  t_updates = {
    counter = 49
  }, 
  t_outstanding_credits = {
    counter = 3605
  }, 
  t_outstanding_revokes = {
    counter = 0
  }, 
  t_handle_count = {
    counter = 347
  }, 
  t_cpnext = 0x0, 
  t_cpprev = 0x0, 
  t_expires = 4507398634, 
  t_start_time = 212726337115951, 
  t_synchronous_commit = 1, 
  t_need_data_flush = 1, 
  t_private_list = {
    next = 0xff1ffc76844dc940, 
    prev = 0xff1ffc786fb63e40
  }
}

Note the t_inode_list.next is already pointing to ff1ffc7b0852aa10
and the jounral_inode itself

crash> struct jbd2_inode ff1ffc7b0852aa00
struct jbd2_inode {
  i_transaction = 0x0, 
  i_next_transaction = 0x0, 
  i_list = {
    next = 0xff1ffc7b0852aa10, 
    prev = 0xff1ffc7b0852aa10
  }, 
  i_vfs_inode = 0xff1ffc71b6eaf240, 
  i_flags = 0, 
  i_dirty_start = 0, 
  i_dirty_end = 0
}

So it's list is pointing to itself, which means it's just been initialized by jbd2_journal_init_jbd_inode().
but the transaction->t_inode_list has already got it and it's the first element, adding it to the list will trigger the crash.

The journal_inode is referring to inode 0xff1ffc71b6eaf240, but it's not in the PID 13293's stack, and it's mentioned in other threads:

crash> search -t ff1ffc71b6eaf240
PID: 13065    TASK: ff1ffc6d552e4000  CPU: 9    COMMAND: "ll_ost_io02_005"
ff3c80102304f398: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102304f4d0: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102304f750: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102304f870: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102304f960: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 

PID: 13265    TASK: ff1ffc70438d4000  CPU: 8    COMMAND: "ll_ost_io02_018"
ff3c801023717408: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717480: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717508: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717540: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717588: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c8010237175e8: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717638: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c8010237177f8: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717880: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c801023717960: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 

PID: 3164759  TASK: ff1ffc6c2c068000  CPU: 9    COMMAND: "ll_ost_io02_064"
ff3c80102ca3f860: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102ca3f890: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102ca3f8d8: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102ca3f8f8: ff1ffc71b6eaf240 
ff3c80102ca3f958: ff1ffc71b6eaf240

out of the three PID 13265 looks interesting.

crash> bt 13265
PID: 13265    TASK: ff1ffc70438d4000  CPU: 8    COMMAND: "ll_ost_io02_018"
 #0 [fffffe00001d3e48] crash_nmi_callback at ffffffff8925e8d3
 #1 [fffffe00001d3e50] nmi_handle at ffffffff8922b393
 #2 [fffffe00001d3ea8] default_do_nmi at ffffffff89bee099
 #3 [fffffe00001d3ec8] do_nmi at ffffffff8922b8ef
 #4 [fffffe00001d3ef0] end_repeat_nmi at ffffffff89e015e8
    [exception RIP: native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath+324]
    RIP: ffffffff8935b264  RSP: ff3c8010237177d0  RFLAGS: 00000246
    RAX: 0000000000000000  RBX: ff1ffc7b0852aa00  RCX: 0000000000000005
    RDX: ff1ffc8e71833d40  RSI: 0000000000240000  RDI: ff1ffc6c117b9ba8
    RBP: ff1ffc779ee5e600   R8: 00000000003fffff   R9: ff1ffc6c117b9ba8
    R10: 0000000000000400  R11: 0000000000000001  R12: 0000000000000000
    R13: 0000000000000000  R14: 00000000003fffff  R15: 0000000000000006
    ORIG_RAX: ffffffffffffffff  CS: 0010  SS: 0018
--- <NMI exception stack> ---
 #5 [ff3c8010237177d0] native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath at ffffffff8935b264
 #6 [ff3c8010237177d0] _raw_spin_lock at ffffffff89c020ce
 #7 [ff3c8010237177d8] jbd2_journal_file_inode at ffffffffc0532fae [jbd2]
 #8 [ff3c801023717820] ldiskfs_map_blocks at ffffffffc15ff337 [ldiskfs]
 #9 [ff3c801023717890] osd_ldiskfs_map_inode_pages at ffffffffc16ff94f [osd_ldiskfs]
#10 [ff3c801023717980] osd_write_commit at ffffffffc1700482 [osd_ldiskfs]
#11 [ff3c801023717a00] ofd_commitrw_write at ffffffffc18485ae [ofd]
#12 [ff3c801023717aa0] ofd_commitrw at ffffffffc184de24 [ofd]
#13 [ff3c801023717b58] obd_commitrw at ffffffffc0d1f9b6 [ptlrpc]
#14 [ff3c801023717bd0] tgt_brw_write at ffffffffc0d2805f [ptlrpc]
#15 [ff3c801023717d50] tgt_request_handle at ffffffffc0d2966d [ptlrpc]
#16 [ff3c801023717dd0] ptlrpc_server_handle_request at ffffffffc0cd4af3 [ptlrpc]
#17 [ff3c801023717e38] ptlrpc_main at ffffffffc0cd660f [ptlrpc]
#18 [ff3c801023717f10] kthread at ffffffff8931d6a4
#19 [ff3c801023717f50] ret_from_fork at ffffffff89e0024f

So looks like thread 13265 initialized the journal_inode in question, and trying to do the same jbd2_journal_file_inode(), but was waiting on the spinlock. and at the same time thread 13293 got the lock,
and somehow calling jbd2_journal_file_inode() with the same journal_inode, plus the journal_inode is already on the list.
Feels like there's a double free somewhere so the journal_inode was still in use and on the list, but got allocated again to 13265 and got the its list re-init.

I've tried to search for that but so far nothing come close.
For now I think it should not have anything to do with lustre as it's all inside jbd2 and lustre/ldiskfs is just using the jbd2 interfaces.
yes, I checked in the dmesg and it ;s 2.15.5_RC1 for this crash
Most likely RC1 - https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_15/86 - but green will check

mdiep in LU-17938 mentioned the crash was seen after some patches reverted, could you also share the lustre packages used at the time of the crash including the debuginfo packages?
Thanks
